title: sisyrinchium adenostemon subsp. microspathum
intro: Sisyrinchium adenostemon subsp. microspathum, also known as blue-eyed grass, is a flowering plant species in the iris family native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and skin conditions. Blue-eyed grass can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further research.
